session.user:
Run these commands in sequence and report ALL failures:
1. First run: ``` cd /home/runner/work/erk/erk && uv run pytest tests/unit/ -x -q --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -40 ```
2. If that passes, run: ``` cd /home/runner/work/erk/erk && uv run pytest tests/commands/ tests/core/ tests/packages/ tests/tui/ -x -q --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -40 ```
Report the complete output of each command. Do NOT modify any files.

tool_use.param: Run unit tests and show last 40 lines

session.assistant:
The first command found a failure. Let me report this result:
**pytest failed: 1 issue found**
**Failure in tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_handle_no_changes.py::test_cli_updates_pr_title_and_body**
Location: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_handle_no_changes.py:280`
Error: AssertionError: assert 2 == 1 - Expected 1 item in `no_changes_bodies` - Found 2 items in `no_changes_bodies`
The test is checking the length of a list containing PR update bodies, expecting exactly 1, but got 2 instead.
Since the first command failed, per your instructions I'm stopping here without running the second command. The failure is in the handle_no_changes test which appears to be checking that the CLI updates PR title and body correctly when no changes occur.
